options: It must be defined in the base class.
It forces the base class to become an Abstract Class.

text: Which of the following is TRUE regarding the 'this' pointer in C++?
options: It is accessible inside static member functions.
It is a constant pointer pointing to the object that invokes the member function.
It can be modified to point to another object.

The exit status of the last command.

text: In graph algorithms, Kruskal's algorithm is primarily used to find:
options: The Shortest Path between two nodes.
The Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) of a connected weighted graph.

text: Which architectural phenomenon limits CPU performance because the shared memory bus cannot fetch data as fast as the CPU can execute instructions?

When an instruction relies on the data output of a previous instruction.
When the pipeline makes wrong decisions on branch prediction, requiring a flush.
When the power supply to the CPU control unit fluctuates.

options: To specify the priority of the packet.

text: Which SQL command is utilized to remove a specific column from an existing table structure?
options: ALTER TABLE table_name DROP COLUMN column_name

text: A database index where the physical order of data rows on the disk perfectly matches the logical order of the index keys is known as a:

options: The path containing tasks with the highest financial cost.
The shortest possible sequence of tasks to complete the project.

text: Which of the following documents acts as a formal contract between the development team and the client regarding what the software will actually do?

In the context of Machine Learning, what does 'Overfitting' mean?
options: The model performs poorly on both training and test data.
The model fails to learn the underlying trend of the data.
The model memorizes the training data, including noise, resulting in poor generalization on new test data.

text: Which of the following scenarios is an example of an 'Unsupervised Learning' task?
options: Predicting house prices based on square footage.
Identifying whether an email is Spam or Not Spam.
Segmenting a large customer base into distinct groups based on purchasing behavior.
Diagnosing a disease from medical images using labeled datasets.

text: What does the `sprintf()` function in C do?

options: The first is for C programs, the second is for C++ programs.
The first searches the standard library directories, the second searches the current directory first.
The first is evaluated at compile time, the second at run time.

text: Can a `switch` case label in C be a floating-point number (e.g., `case 1.5:`)?
options: Yes, as long as it is a double.
Yes, but it will be automatically truncated to an integer.
No, switch cases can only be integral or character constants.
No, switch cases can only be enumerations.

To allow the function to be called from a different namespace.

text: How does the C-LOOK disk scheduling algorithm behave upon reaching the last request in the current direction?
options: It reverses direction and services requests on the way back.
It travels all the way to the physical edge of the disk platter before returning.
It jumps back to the furthest request in the opposite direction without servicing any requests on the return trip.
It stops permanently until a new request arrives.
